title: Extract the first name (Snowflake REGEXP_SUBSTR)
prompt: You're writing Snowflake SQL. Customer names are stored as 'First Last'. Using Snowflake's REGEXP_SUBSTR, extract just the first name (the run of letters before the space) for each customer. Columns: name, first_name. Order by name.

requires.message: Use Snowflake's REGEXP_SUBSTR here, not a different string function.

hints: REGEXP_SUBSTR(name, '^[A-Za-z]+') returns the leading run of letters — the first name.
The ^ anchors the match to the start of the string.
